Scenario.overview: Energy Intermediate The 2015 Ukraine Electric Power Attack involved a sophisticated phishing campaign targeting energy sector employees, aiming to disrupt power supply by gaining unauthorized access to industrial control systems.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Employees, Internet-facing assets, Shared DB or directory, Web server services such as emails and databases
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Email, Web, Phishing, Social engineering

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2022-43939 [CWE-22]: Microsoft Exchange Server Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
stage.description: The Sandworm group sent spear-phishing emails to energy sector employees, exploiting a vulnerability in Microsoft Exchange Server to gain initial access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2024-55550 [CWE-78]: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Statement ('SQL Injection')
stage.description: Upon successful login, malicious scripts were executed via web browsers, exploiting a SQL injection vulnerability to achieve remote code execution.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 70 - Try Common or Default Usernames and Passwords
stage.description: The attackers created new accounts with elevated privileges in shared directories, ensuring persistence within the network.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Using stolen credentials, attackers exploited vulnerabilities in web applications to escalate privileges and move laterally within the network.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 636 - Hiding Malicious Data or Code within Files
stage.description: To evade detection, attackers hid malicious files within legitimate ones and employed obfuscation techniques to conceal their activities.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Attackers disabled security software and tools to facilitate further movement within the network and prevent detection.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 675 - Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Finally, the attackers caused power outages by manipulating industrial control systems, demonstrating their ability to cause significant impact.

Scenario.summary: The 2015 Ukraine Electric Power Attack showcased the Sandworm group's advanced capabilities, successfully infiltrating and disrupting critical energy infrastructure through a combination of phishing, exploitation, and lateral movement techniques.
